title: The One Where Heckles Dies

jokes.text: When she sneezed, bats flew out of them.
jokes.explanation: Strong visual absurdity with good economy - classic Chandler exaggeration style

jokes.text: I'm telling you, she leaned back, I could see her brain.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but less original than the bats line - relies on similar absurdity

jokes.text: Joey, women don't have Adam's apples.
jokes.setup: Joey's story about girlfriend's Adam's apple
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey character moment - his confusion about basic anatomy is very much in character

jokes.text: Heh, heh. You guys are messing with me, right?
jokes.setup: Learning women don't have Adam's apples
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey confusion beat - functional but predictable

jokes.text: That's a good one. For a second there, I was like, whoa!
jokes.explanation: Weak payoff - Joey's ignorance is established but resolution lacks punch

jokes.text: Because she doesn't hate Yanni is not a real reason.
jokes.setup: Ross naming Maureen Rasillo as legitimate breakup
jokes.explanation: Good specificity with Yanni reference - captures 90s dating absurdity

jokes.text: You don't have birds. / I could have birds.
jokes.setup: Heckles complaining about disturbing his birds
jokes.punchline: Admission he doesn't have birds but could
jokes.explanation: Perfect Heckles logic - absurd but delivered with complete conviction. Establishes his eccentric character beautifully

jokes.text: I'm going to rejoin my dinner party.
jokes.setup: Heckles living alone and being antisocial
jokes.punchline: Pretending to have a dinner party
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Heckles' delusional social life

jokes.text: Hello, Chandler Bing! Oh, my God!
jokes.punchline: Spot-on Janice impression with her catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Standard Janice impression - familiar callback but well-executed

jokes.text: Oh, Chandler! Now! Now! That's it. There! Faster!
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo disguised as Janice impression
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - starts as character impression, becomes sexual innuendo

jokes.text: We won! / Mr. Heckles.
jokes.setup: Celebrating victory over Heckles' noise complaints
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy timing - celebration immediately undercut by tragedy

jokes.text: I was sweeping yesterday. It could've been me.
jokes.punchline: Joey's illogical fear about sweeping danger
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic - missing the real cause of death, focusing on irrelevant detail

jokes.text: Go into the light, Mr. Heckles!
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe spiritual nonsense delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Like crop circles or the Bermuda Triangle or evolution.
jokes.setup: Listing things she doesn't believe in
jokes.punchline: Including established scientific fact with conspiracy theories
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - puts science on same level as pseudoscience, very Phoebe

jokes.text: Monkeys, Darwin, it's a nice story. I just think it's a little too easy.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - calling millions of years of evolution 'too easy'. Perfect Phoebe logic

jokes.text: Okay, don't get me started on gravity.
jokes.setup: Ross invoking gravity as undeniable fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy - one line sets up entire absurd worldview. Highly quotable

jokes.text: Lately I get the feeling that I'm not so much being pulled down as I am being pushed.
jokes.punchline: Alternative gravity theory with mysterious pushers
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific absurdity - Phoebe has thought this through in her own way

jokes.text: Uh-oh. It's Isaac Newton and he's pissed.
jokes.setup: Knock at door during gravity discussion
jokes.punchline: Perfect timing reference to gravity's discoverer
jokes.explanation: Excellent timing and reference - perfect Chandler quip with great economy

jokes.text: According my client's will, he wants to leave all his earthly possessions to 'the noisy girls in the apartment above mine.'
jokes.punchline: Heckles' backhanded description of Monica and Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established dynamic with nice character touch

jokes.text: All right, there was none.
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection but effective - quick deflation of expectations

jokes.text: He hated us! This was his final revenge! / Ever seen so much crap! Actually, I think this apartment sullies the good name of crap.
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler voice with creative escalation - 'sullies the good name of crap' is clever

jokes.text: So now the real question is, who put those fossils there and why?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe logic - accepts evidence but creates bigger conspiracy. Character-perfect

jokes.text: April 18th, excessive noise, Italian guy's gay roommate brings home dry cleaning.
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Heckles' obsessive documentation of normal activities

jokes.text: You don't have any stuff. / You still think of it as your apartment, don't you?
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict moment - Monica's controlling nature vs Rachel's assertion

jokes.text: Maybe the Overlords needed them to steer their spacecrafts.
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific Phoebe worldview - 'Overlords' is a great touch

jokes.text: I think maybe it's time you put Ross under the microscope.
jokes.punchline: Scientific metaphor turned back on Ross
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay using Ross's own scientific language against him

jokes.text: Wow. Heckles was voted class clown, and so was I.
jokes.explanation: Sets up major character arc for episode - Chandler's existential crisis

jokes.text: Well, there was no club, but I sure thought they were cool.
jokes.setup: Scale modeler's club connection with Heckles
jokes.explanation: Good character depth moment - shows Chandler's social isolation tendency

jokes.text: So? You were both dorks. Big deal.
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey missing the point completely - good character contrast

jokes.text: Vivian, too tall. / Madge, big gums. / Too loud. / Too smart. / Makes noise when she eats.
jokes.setup: Looking at Heckles' photos and notes
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Chandler's future - each complaint more petty than last

jokes.text: Bitter Town, Alone-ville, Hermit Junction!
jokes.setup: Train metaphor for his life path
jokes.punchline: Creative place names for lonely destinations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay with great rhythm - highly quotable Chandler line

jokes.text: What if I found her but I dumped her because she pronounces it 'supposably'?
jokes.punchline: Specific grammar pet peeve as relationship ender
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler neurosis - very specific and relatable pickiness

jokes.text: I mean, what if we're over at her folks' place?
jokes.explanation: Realistic but uncomfortable moment - Joey's practical concern vs friendship

jokes.text: Supposably. / Supposably. / Did they go to the zoo? / Supposably.
jokes.punchline: Obsessive repetition of grammar pet peeve
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Chandler's obsessive nature - practice makes it funnier

jokes.text: Jeez, look how fat she got.
jokes.explanation: Typical shallow Chandler reaction but lacks originality - setup for bigger reveal

jokes.text: Is it yours? Ha! You wish, Chandler Bing.
jokes.explanation: Good Janice character moment - appropriately dismissive of Chandler

jokes.text: And what? Miss the expression on your face? Oh, no. Janice likes to have her fun.
jokes.setup: Chandler asking why she didn't tell him on phone
jokes.punchline: Janice admitting she enjoyed his pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Janice cruelty - she knows exactly what she's doing to Chandler

jokes.text: Then you'll just have to eat the other lamps.
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay attempt - doesn't land clearly, seems forced

jokes.text: And up till 50 years ago, you all thought the atom was the smallest thing until you split it open and this whole mess of crap came out.
jokes.punchline: Describing atomic particles as 'mess of crap'
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe voice - reduces complex physics to her terminology

jokes.text: There might be a teeny, tiny possibility.
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - scientist forced to acknowledge uncertainty

jokes.text: How are you gonna face the other science guys?
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler observation - 'science guys' is appropriately casual

jokes.text: I was putting on my jacket and the thing and the lamp and it broke.
jokes.explanation: Standard bad lying comedy - functional but unremarkable

jokes.text: I didn't see it, because I was putting on my jacket. But I want to believe you.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe diplomacy - wants to be supportive but can't quite lie

jokes.text: Like that guy on the subway who eats his own face.
jokes.setup: Needing a 'thing' as lonely old man
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and disturbing - classic NYC weird character reference

jokes.text: Run away from Crazy Snake Man, they'll shout!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the snake man fantasy - Chandler's self-deprecating vision

jokes.text: Chandler, you have just described virtually every man that we've ever gone out with.
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out universality of male behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about dating patterns - relatable and true

jokes.text: Whoa! Don't know about that.
jokes.setup: Friends saying he's ready for commitment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate panic at the word 'commitment'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler commitment phobia - predictable but in character

jokes.text: Clock whistles
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag - immediate consequence for Monica's acceptance

jokes.text: But I'm not going to let that stuff hang me up anymore because she has an unusually large head.
jokes.setup: Chandler announcing growth and new date
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback showing Chandler hasn't learned - says he's growing while demonstrating he hasn't

jokes.text: You're all going to hell.
jokes.setup: Everyone laughing during Heckles memorial moment
jokes.explanation: Good Phoebe spiritual moment - appropriate moral judgment delivery

jokes.text: My God, that's a big head! It didn't look this big in the office. Maybe it's the lighting. My head must look like a golf ball at work.
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Chandler's obsessive mind - the golf ball comparison is inspired

jokes.text: Nice smile, good dresser. Big head, big head, big head!
jokes.punchline: Obsession overwhelming his attempt at positivity
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Chandler's inability to move past physical flaws - great rhythm

tmdb_overview: After Mr. Heckles passes away leaving belongings to Monica and Rachel, Chandler notices similarities to his own life. Ross and Phoebe debate evolution.
